description: A 20-pattern curriculum that teaches the most common tactical themes in Cờ tướng. Separate from Practice mode (random puzzles). Patterns is a structured course — you work through it in order because each concept builds on the last. Connected to the coaching engine: when you fall for a pattern in a real game, the app nudges you back here.

menu_position.description: Sits between Learn (piece movement tutorials) and Practice (random puzzles). Learn teaches rules. Patterns teaches concepts. Practice tests recall. Play applies everything.

level_1.name: Survival
level_1.subtitle: Stop getting killed by these
level_1.focus: Defensive — recognize traps and escape them
level_1.unlock: Available immediately after completing Learn tutorial

level_2.name: Attack
level_2.subtitle: Learn to deliver these
level_2.focus: Offensive — find and create tactical combinations
level_2.unlock: Available after completing all 10 Survival patterns

structure.sequential_unlock: Patterns unlock one at a time within each level. Complete pattern 3 to unlock pattern 4.
structure.exception: If coaching engine detects a specific pattern in a real game loss, that pattern unlocks early with a prompt: 'You just fell for this in your game. Want to learn it now?'

per_pattern_flow.description: Each pattern follows a 3-step learning flow. Takes about 5-8 minutes total.

steps.name: See It
steps.description: Watch the pattern play out on the board. 4-6 moves, auto-animated with pause/replay controls.

content.board: Standard board component loaded with a FEN position
content.animation: Moves play automatically at 1.5 second intervals
content.annotations: Each move has a text callout explaining what's happening and why
content.controls: Play/pause, step forward, step back, restart

steps.name: Spot It
steps.description: 3 puzzles where you recognize the pattern and find the winning/saving move. Engine-validated (multiple correct solutions accepted).
steps.validation: Run minimax on position after player's move. If eval confirms the pattern is exploited/escaped correctly, move is accepted.
steps.difficulty_curve: Puzzle 1 is obvious, puzzle 2 has a slight twist, puzzle 3 requires seeing one move deeper

steps.name: Survive It / Deliver It
steps.description: 2 puzzles from the opposite perspective. Survival patterns: you're the one escaping. Attack patterns: you're building the setup from scratch.
steps.difficulty_curve: Harder than Spot It — requires planning 2-3 moves ahead

completion.all_5_puzzles_solved: Pattern marked complete, next pattern unlocks
completion.replay: Completed patterns can be replayed anytime for review

level_1_survival.name: Opening Cannon Trap
level_1_survival.concept: Cannons can attack your Horses from move one using a piece as a screen. This is the most common way beginners lose material in the opening.

see_it.moves: 4-5 move sequence showing Central Cannon opening attacking an undeveloped Horse
annotations: Red moves Cannon to center file — this is the most popular opening in Cờ tướng
The Cannon now has a screen (the Soldier on the same file)
Black's Horse on b10 is a target — it's sitting on the Cannon's attack line
If Black doesn't develop the Elephant to block the screen, the Horse is lost

spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Your Horse is under attack from the Cannon. Find the Elephant move that blocks the screen.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: The Cannon has two possible screens. Which piece should you move to remove both threats?
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_3: The opponent set up the Central Cannon. You have one move to protect your Horse — but one option walks into a different trap.

survive_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Your Horse is already attacked and can't be saved. Find the counter-threat that wins material back.
survive_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: You've lost one Horse to the Cannon trap. Reorganize your remaining pieces to prevent losing the second Horse.

level_1_survival.name: Accidental Screen
level_1_survival.concept: You move a piece into a line between the opponent's Cannon and your General (or another valuable piece), accidentally creating a screen for the Cannon to capture.

annotations: Black's Cannon is on the same file as Red's General, but there's no screen — safe for now
Red moves a Horse to a central position for development
That Horse is now sitting between the Cannon and the General — it's a screen
The Cannon captures the General through the screen — checkmate

spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: You want to move your Horse forward. Will this create a screen for the enemy Cannon? Find the safe square instead.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: The enemy Cannon is aiming at your General. Two of your pieces could move — which one doesn't create a screen?
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_3: You need to develop your Elephant. One diagonal creates a Cannon screen, the other doesn't. Find the safe path.

survive_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: You accidentally created a screen. The Cannon will capture next move. Find the only escape.
survive_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Your Advisor became a screen for the enemy Cannon. Move a piece to break the screen line before it's too late.

coaching_engine_link.trigger: Player moves a piece that becomes an opponent's Cannon screen

level_1_survival.name: Flying General Pin
level_1_survival.concept: A piece standing on the file between the two Generals cannot move — it's pinned by the Flying General rule. Moving it would leave the Generals facing each other, which is illegal.

annotations: Both Generals are on the same file (the e-file)
Red's Horse is the only piece between them
The Horse CANNOT move — any move exposes the Generals to each other
Black's Chariot captures the pinned Horse for free — it can't run

spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Your opponent's Chariot is pinned by the Flying General rule. Capture it for free.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Which of your pieces is pinned? Identify it before moving.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_3: Your Cannon is pinned on the e-file. Find a way to move your General off the file to unpin it.

survive_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Your Horse is pinned and under attack. Move your General sideways to release the pin before the Horse is captured.
survive_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Two of your pieces are on the same file as both Generals. Move one piece to break the pin on the other.

coaching_engine_link.trigger: Player attempts to move a piece pinned by Flying General rule

level_1_survival.name: The Sneaky Elephant
level_1_survival.concept: The Elephant looks passive and defensive, but it controls key diagonals on its side of the board. Moving a piece into its capture zone is a common beginner mistake.

annotations: Black's Elephant on c8 looks like it's just sitting there
Red advances a Horse to a strong-looking central square
That square is exactly 2 diagonal steps from the Elephant
The Elephant captures the Horse — a piece Red forgot could even reach that square

spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: You want to move your Horse forward. Is the destination square in the enemy Elephant's capture range? Find the safe alternative.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: The enemy Elephant's eye is blocked. Is it safe to move into its diagonal now? Verify before moving.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_3: Two squares look equally good for your Cannon. One is in the Elephant's range, one isn't. Find the safe one.

survive_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Your piece is under attack by the Elephant. Capture the Elephant or move to safety.
survive_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Block the Elephant's eye to prevent it from capturing your advanced piece.

coaching_engine_link.trigger: Player moves a piece to a square that is immediately captured by an Elephant

level_1_survival.name: Hanging Piece
level_1_survival.concept: A piece with no defender is a free capture for the opponent. Before every move, ask: is the piece I'm moving to still protected? And does my move leave another piece unprotected?

annotations: Red moves a Chariot forward to attack Black's Soldier
But Red's Chariot was also defending a Horse on the other side of the board
Now the Horse is unprotected — hanging
Black captures the Horse for free. Red won a Soldier but lost a Horse — terrible trade.

spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: The opponent has a hanging piece. Find the free capture.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: You want to move your Chariot. Does this leave any of your other pieces unprotected? Find the safe move.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_3: Two enemy pieces are hanging. Which one should you capture first? (One has a hidden defender.)

survive_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: You just realized your Horse is hanging. Defend it, move it, or create a bigger counter-threat.
survive_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: You have two hanging pieces. You can only save one. Which is more valuable?

coaching_engine_link.trigger: Player moves a piece to an undefended square where it is captured next move

level_1_survival.name: Exposed General
level_1_survival.concept: Trading away both Advisors or pulling them out of position leaves your General vulnerable to Cannon attacks and Chariot invasions. Keep at least one Advisor in the palace.

annotations: Red trades both Advisors for Black's minor pieces — seems equal on material
But now the General has no bodyguards in the palace
Black's Cannon aims at the palace — with no Advisor to block, it's check
The General has nowhere to hide — checkmate

spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: The opponent offers to trade your last Advisor. Should you accept? Evaluate the resulting position.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Your General is exposed. Find the defensive move that blocks the Cannon's attack line.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_3: Both your Advisors are on the edge of the palace. Reposition one to protect the General from a file attack.

survive_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Both Advisors are gone. Find the only piece that can block the Cannon's checkmate.
survive_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Your General is in check with no Advisors. Use a piece to interpose and create a counter-threat simultaneously.

coaching_engine_link.trigger: General has no Advisor support when under attack

level_1_survival.name: Horse Leg Blindspot
level_1_survival.concept: The Horse's leg-block rule is the most commonly forgotten rule for beginners from Western chess. A piece adjacent to the Horse in the orthogonal direction blocks that L-jump.

annotations: Red's Horse on e5 looks like it can jump to f7 to capture Black's Cannon
But there's a Soldier on e6 — that's the Horse's 'leg' for the northward jump
The Horse is blocked in that direction
Red must either move the blocking Soldier first or find a different plan

spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Your Horse has 8 theoretical destinations. How many are actually legal? Tap all reachable squares.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: You need your Horse on a specific square to deliver check. It's blocked. Find the move that clears the leg.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_3: The opponent's Horse looks like it threatens your piece. Is the leg actually blocked? Verify before panicking.

survive_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Block the enemy Horse's leg to prevent it from capturing your piece next move.
survive_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Your Horse is surrounded by pieces. Find the one move that unblocks the most useful jumping direction.

coaching_engine_link.trigger: Player attempts a Horse move that is blocked by the leg rule

level_1_survival.name: Elephant Eye Blindspot
level_1_survival.concept: The Elephant moves exactly 2 diagonals, but a piece on the midpoint ('eye') blocks the move entirely. Similar to the Horse leg-block but for Elephants.

annotations: Red's Elephant on c1 wants to move to a3 to defend the left flank
But there's a Soldier on b2 — that's the Elephant's 'eye'
The Elephant can't make that diagonal jump
Red must move the blocking piece first or use a different Elephant route

spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Your Elephant has 4 theoretical destinations. Which are blocked by eye pieces? Tap only the legal ones.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: You need the Elephant on a specific defensive square. The eye is blocked. Find the clearing move.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_3: The opponent moved a piece into your Elephant's eye. Is this accidental or a deliberate strategy to weaken your defense?

survive_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Block the enemy Elephant's eye to prevent it from reaching a defensive position.
survive_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Your Elephant is stuck because both useful eyes are blocked. Reroute through a different path.

coaching_engine_link.trigger: Player attempts an Elephant move that is blocked by the eye rule

level_1_survival.name: Premature Attack
level_1_survival.concept: Attacking with one or two pieces while the rest of your army is undeveloped. The attack fails and you've wasted moves while your opponent developed freely.

annotations: Red sends a Cannon on an adventure — moving it 3 times in the opening
Meanwhile Black develops Horse, Elephant, Chariot — three different pieces
Red's Cannon attacks are easily blocked because Black has more pieces in play
Red is now behind in development with a lonely Cannon and no support

spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: You have two options: attack with your Cannon (already moved twice) or develop your Horse. Which is better?
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: The opponent has only developed one piece aggressively. Find the developing move that also defends the threat.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_3: Count: how many of your pieces have moved? How many of the opponent's? If you're behind on development, find the developing move.

survive_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: You've overextended your Cannon and it's now trapped. Find the retreat that saves it while developing another piece.
survive_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Your opponent has attacked prematurely. Punish their underdevelopment by opening a line to their undefended back rank.

coaching_engine_link.trigger: Same piece moved 3+ times in the first 8 moves while other pieces remain on starting squares

level_1_survival.name: Repetition Trap
level_1_survival.concept: Getting stuck in a cycle where both sides repeat the same moves. In Cờ tướng, the chasing side must break the cycle or lose. Knowing when you're the chaser vs the defender matters.

annotations: Red's Chariot checks Black's General. Black moves the General back.
Red checks again from a different angle. Black retreats again.
This repeats — Red is perpetually checking
Under Cờ tướng rules, Red (the chaser) must stop or forfeit. Black is fine.

spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: You're checking the General repeatedly. Is there a move that breaks the cycle and actually checkmates instead?
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: The opponent is chasing your piece back and forth. You don't need to do anything — they must stop. But can you find a counter-attack while they're forced to break the cycle?
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_3: Both sides are repeating. Who is the chaser? Who must break the cycle?

survive_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: You've been perpetually checking and must stop. Find the non-check move that maintains your advantage.
survive_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: The opponent is forcing a repetition to save a lost position. Find the winning move that avoids the cycle entirely.

coaching_engine_link.trigger: Same position appears 3 times in a game

level_2_attack.name: General's Smile
level_2_attack.concept: Use the Flying General rule as an offensive weapon. Clear the file between the two Generals — your General 'attacks' along the open file, supporting a piece to deliver checkmate.

annotations: Both Generals are on the e-file with one piece between them
Red moves that piece away — now the Generals face each other
But wait — Red's Soldier is right next to the Black General
The Soldier delivers check, and the General can't capture it because it's 'protected' by the Red General across the board — checkmate

spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Clear the file to use your General as support. Find the checkmate.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Move a piece off the e-file to create the General's Smile — but which piece? One move checkmates, the other doesn't.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_3: Your General and the enemy General are on the same file with two pieces between them. Remove one to create a devastating pin.

deliver_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Set up the General's Smile from scratch. First move your General to the same file, then clear the path.
deliver_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: The Generals are on different files. Maneuver in 3 moves to create the Smile checkmate.

level_2_attack.name: Double Cannons
level_2_attack.concept: Two Cannons on the same file. The front Cannon is the screen for the back Cannon. Devastating and hard to defend against.

annotations: Red places both Cannons on the e-file, one behind the other
The front Cannon serves as the screen for the back Cannon
Black's General is at the end of the file — the back Cannon delivers check through the front Cannon as screen
Even if Black blocks, the front Cannon can capture the blocker, maintaining the attack

spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Your two Cannons are on the same file. Find the checkmate.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: One Cannon is in position. Find where to place the second Cannon to create the Double Cannon attack.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_3: The opponent blocked your Double Cannon with an Advisor. Capture the Advisor with the front Cannon — does the checkmate still work?

deliver_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Your Cannons are on different files. Reposition them onto the same file targeting the General in 2 moves.
deliver_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Create the Double Cannon setup while also blocking the opponent's counter-attack.

level_2_attack.name: Smothered Cannon Mate
level_2_attack.concept: The Cannon delivers checkmate because the General is surrounded by its own pieces and can't escape. The General is 'smothered' by its own army.

annotations: Black's General is boxed in by its own Advisors and Elephants
Red's Cannon uses one of Black's own pieces as a screen
Check — and the General has no escape because its own pieces block every exit
Checkmate. Black's defense became its own prison.

spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: The enemy General is smothered. Find the Cannon move that delivers mate.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: The General has one escape square. Drive a piece there first to close the escape, then deliver the Smothered Cannon mate.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_3: Force the opponent to block their own General's escape by making a threat they must respond to with an Advisor.

deliver_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: The General isn't smothered yet. Force the opponent to block their own escape in 2 moves, then deliver mate.
deliver_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Sacrifice a piece to force the opponent's Advisor into a blocking position, then smother with the Cannon.

level_2_attack.name: Horse-Cannon Checkmate
level_2_attack.concept: The Horse controls the General's escape squares while the Cannon delivers check from behind. Classic combination — the Horse does the dirty work, the Cannon finishes.

annotations: Red's Horse is on d8 — right next to Black's palace
The Horse controls e10 and f9 — two of the General's escape squares
Red's Cannon fires down the e-file with a screen — check
The General can't move to the squares the Horse covers — checkmate

spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Your Horse is already next to the palace. Find the Cannon move that checkmates.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Your Cannon is in position but the Horse isn't. Move the Horse to the right square to complete the checkmate.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_3: Set up the Horse-Cannon mate, but the Horse's leg is blocked. Clear the leg first.

deliver_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Neither piece is in position. Maneuver both into the Horse-Cannon checkmate in 3 moves.
deliver_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Sacrifice your Chariot to distract the defense, then deliver the Horse-Cannon mate.

level_2_attack.name: Chariot Back-Rank Mate
level_2_attack.concept: The Chariot drives to the enemy's back rank and delivers checkmate. The General is trapped in the palace with no escape.

annotations: Red's Chariot is on an open file with no obstacles
It drives all the way to the last rank — right next to Black's palace
The General is trapped — Advisors block its escape on the diagonals
Chariot delivers checkmate on the back rank

spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: The back rank is open. Drive your Chariot in for checkmate.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: One piece blocks your Chariot's path to the back rank. Capture it first, then checkmate.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_3: The back rank has a defender. Sacrifice a piece to remove the defender, then deliver the back-rank mate.

deliver_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Open a file for your Chariot by trading off the piece that's blocking it, then checkmate on the back rank.
deliver_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Your Chariot is on the back rank but the General has an escape. Use another piece to cover the escape, then deliver mate.

level_2_attack.name: Horse Fork
level_2_attack.concept: The Horse attacks two valuable pieces at once. The opponent can only save one. You win material.

annotations: Red's Horse jumps to a central square
From this position it simultaneously attacks Black's Chariot on one side and Black's Cannon on the other
Black can only move one piece — the other is captured
Red wins material through the fork

spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Find the square where your Horse forks two enemy pieces.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Your Horse can fork, but its leg is blocked. Clear the leg and deliver the fork next move.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_3: The opponent's General and Chariot are both vulnerable. Find the Horse fork that attacks both.

deliver_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Set up the fork in 2 moves. First threaten something to force the opponent's piece to a forkable square, then fork.
deliver_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Sacrifice a Soldier to lure the enemy Chariot to a square where your Horse forks it with the General.

level_2_attack.name: Chariot-Cannon Battery
level_2_attack.concept: Chariot and Cannon on the same file or rank, working together. The Chariot can serve as the Cannon's screen, and both attack the same target. Extremely powerful combination.

annotations: Red's Chariot and Cannon are both on the e-file
The Chariot advances to attack — and it's also now a screen for the Cannon behind it
Black must deal with both threats simultaneously
The Chariot captures a defender, and now the Cannon has a clear shot at the General

spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Your Chariot and Cannon are on the same file. Find the Chariot move that creates a double threat.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Align your Cannon behind your Chariot on the same file. What threat does this create?
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_3: The opponent has a Chariot-Cannon battery aimed at you. Break the battery by removing the screen.

deliver_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Place your Chariot and Cannon on the same file in 2 moves to create an unstoppable attack.
deliver_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Use the Chariot-Cannon battery to force checkmate. The Chariot sacrifices itself to create a screen for the Cannon's killing blow.

level_2_attack.name: Crossed-River Soldier Mate
level_2_attack.concept: A Soldier that has crossed the river can move forward and sideways. Near the palace, it becomes a serious mating threat — especially with support from the Flying General rule.

annotations: Red's Soldier has crossed the river and advanced deep into Black's territory
It's right next to the palace — moving sideways to deliver check
The General can't capture it because the Red General supports it via the Flying General rule
The Soldier — the weakest piece — delivers checkmate

spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Your Soldier is next to the enemy palace. Find the checkmate.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Two Soldiers have crossed the river. Which one should advance to deliver mate?
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_3: Your Soldier can check the General, but is it actually checkmate or can the General escape? Verify before committing.

deliver_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Your Soldier is 2 moves from the palace. Advance it for a checkmate while using the Flying General for support.
deliver_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Sacrifice your Chariot to clear the path for the Soldier to deliver the final blow.

level_2_attack.name: Clearance Sacrifice
level_2_attack.concept: Sacrifice a piece to clear a file, rank, or diagonal for another piece to deliver a decisive attack. The sacrifice looks like a mistake, but it opens the killing lane.

annotations: Red's own Soldier is blocking the Chariot's path to the back rank
Red advances the Soldier into a capture — Black takes it, thinking it's a free piece
The file is now open — Red's Chariot drives straight to the back rank
Checkmate. The 'free' Soldier was bait to clear the line.

spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Your Chariot is blocked by your own piece. Sacrifice it to open the file, then checkmate.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: A piece is blocking your Cannon's screen line. Move it into a sacrifice that also creates a threat.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_3: Find the counter-intuitive sacrifice that loses material but forces checkmate in 2.

deliver_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Plan a 3-move sequence: sacrifice to clear, attack to penetrate, checkmate to finish.
deliver_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: The position looks equal. Find the sacrifice that turns it into a forced win.

level_2_attack.name: Double Check
level_2_attack.concept: Two pieces deliver check at the same time. The General can't capture both, can't block both, and must move. If there's nowhere to move — checkmate.

annotations: Red moves a piece that was blocking a Cannon's line — discovered check from the Cannon
But the piece that moved ALSO delivers check — it's a Horse landing next to the palace
Two checks at once. Black can't block both.
The General has no escape square — double check, checkmate.

spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Find the move that delivers double check and checkmate.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Your Cannon is aimed at the General but blocked by your own Horse. Move the Horse to a square that also delivers check.
spot_it_puzzles.puzzle_3: Set up a discovered check from the Chariot while the moved piece also checks. Two checks, no escape.

deliver_it_puzzles.puzzle_1: Position your pieces over 2 moves to create the double check scenario on move 3.
deliver_it_puzzles.puzzle_2: Sacrifice a piece to force the General to a square where your double check works. Find the whole sequence.

puzzle_generation.description: Each pattern needs 5 puzzle positions. 20 patterns × 5 puzzles = 100 puzzles total.
puzzle_generation.method: Use minimax engine to validate all puzzles. Each puzzle must have at least one forced winning solution at the required depth. Multiple correct solutions are accepted — engine validates by checking if the resulting position maintains the winning advantage.
puzzle_generation.fen_format: Standard Cờ tướng FEN for each starting position
puzzle_generation.initial_generation: Use Claude CLI to generate candidate positions for each pattern, then validate with the game engine. Discard positions with no clean solution.
puzzle_generation.future_expansion: Pattern tracker can identify real game positions that match each pattern category and add them as bonus puzzles.

pattern_selector.layout: Two sections — Survival (1-10) and Attack (11-20). Vertical scrolling list.

per_pattern_card.content: Pattern number, name, Vietnamese name, lock/unlock icon, progress (0/5 puzzles solved)
per_pattern_card.locked_style: Grayed out with lock icon. Tap shows: 'Complete pattern {n-1} to unlock'
per_pattern_card.unlocked_style: Full color with pattern name and progress bar
per_pattern_card.completed_style: Gold border or checkmark, shows 'Completed' with option to replay

see_it_screen.layout: Board with animated moves + annotation text below in a scrollable area
see_it_screen.controls: Play/Pause, Step Forward, Step Back, Restart

puzzle_screen.layout: Same as Practice mode puzzle screen — board + prompt + hint/skip buttons

coaching_engine_integration.description: The coaching engine's pattern tracker maps directly to these 20 patterns. When a tracked pattern fires, it links the player to the corresponding Pattern lesson.
coaching_engine_integration.flow: Player loses a game → post-game analysis detects 'early_horse_loss' → pattern tracker notes it → post-game card says: 'You fell for the Opening Cannon Trap again. Practice it?' → tap goes to Pattern #1
coaching_engine_integration.early_unlock: If a pattern is detected in a real game but hasn't been unlocked yet in the progression, it unlocks early. Learning from your own mistakes takes priority over sequential order.

dependencies: Game engine with minimax for puzzle validation — DONE
Puzzle interaction (tap to select, tap to move, engine responds) — DONE (from Practice mode)
Coaching engine pattern tracker — from coaching_engine_spec
new_code_needed: Pattern data loader — load pattern JSON with see_it animations and puzzle FENs
See It animation player — auto-play moves with annotations on the existing board
Pattern selector UI with lock/unlock state
Sequential unlock logic — track completion in player profile
Early unlock hook from coaching engine pattern tracker
implementation_notes.estimated_effort: 1.5 weeks. The board, puzzle interaction, and engine are all reusable. Main work is creating the 100 puzzle positions and the See It animations.
